studies.risk_class: Risk Class I according to MDR
studies.duration_additional_info: The programme lasted for 8 weeks, with pre-post training comparisons immediately after the training and 3 months later.
studies.additional_info_about_trial_arms: Intervention group (IG) received access to an 8-week online training tool for fatigue management. Control group (CG) was a wait-list group that received information about the option to enter the FATIMA programme after the study.
studies.collected_data: 1. Fatigue: Chalder Fatigue Scale (Cella & Chalder et al., 2010), FSMC (Penner et al., 2009). 2. Anxiety and depression: Hospital Anxiety and Depression Scale (HADS, Zigmond & Snaith, 1983). 3. Quality of life: Hamburg Quality of Life Questionnaire in MS (HAQUAMS, Gold et al., 2001). 4. Cognitive self-assessment: Multiple Sclerosis Neuropsychological Screening Questionnaire (MSNQ, Benedict et al., 2003). Exploratory: 5. Cognitive and behavioural responses to symptoms questionnaire (Skerett & Moss-Morris, 2006). 6. Brief Illness Perception Questionnaire (Broadbent et al., 2006). 7. Coping Self Efficacy Scale (CSES, Chesney et al., 2006). 8. Activity of daily life: Frenchay Activity Index (Turnbull et al., 2000). 9. Personality: Revised NEO Personality Inventory (Costa & McCrae, 1992).

studies.risk_class: Risk Class I according to MDR
studies.additional_info_about_trial_arms: Intervention group received a 12-week online fatigue management programme (ELEVIDA) based on CBT and mindfulness principles. Control group was a waitlist control.
studies.collected_data: Chalder Fatigue Scale scores assessed at baseline, week 12 (postintervention), and week 24 (follow-up).
studies.key_outcomes_findings: Significantly greater reductions in Chalder Fatigue Scale scores were observed in the ELEVIDA group at week 12 (between-group mean difference 2.74 points; 95% CI 1.16 to 4.32; p=0.0007; effect size d=0.53), with effects sustained at week 24 (between-group mean difference 2.19 points; 95% CI 0.57 to 3.82; p=0.0080).

audit.study_title: Online therapy programme for fatigue in multiple sclerosis
audit.note: registry-only: outcome measures captured under collected_data; no reported results

audit.study_title: Randomised controlled trial of a self-guided online fatigue intervention in multiple sclerosis.
